target_inquiry: effect of city platform exposure on firm green patent count
who_where_when: Chinese cities and linked firms with auditable platform rollout dates during the declared panel window.
scope_logic: The exposure concept is meaningful only when rollout timing can be linked to the unit before outcome measurement.
boundary_failure_mode: If rollout timing or unit linkage is not auditable, exposure becomes a generic digitalization label and the mechanism cannot be tested.
observable_implication: The data audit should show rollout date coverage, unit linkage, and pre-outcome timing for exposed units.
required_gate: Workflow must gate whether the paper claims only audited-rollout scope or a broader platform-governance scope.

target_inquiry: effect of city platform exposure on firm green patent count
who_where_when: Cities outside the audited rollout and linkage window should remain outside the initial theory claim.
scope_logic: The mechanism needs observable administrative-service change and linked innovation outcomes at the same unit grain.
boundary_failure_mode: If the source base only supports descriptive rollout narratives, theory contribution and mechanism strength remain required gates.
observable_implication: The workbench should route broad external-validity language to required gate rather than .aiss model update.
required_gate: Workflow must gate whether this is a theory scope condition or a data availability limit.
